# Oriel

> Oriel is a fast, local, single-binary web GUI for Colima and Docker, a free, open-source Docker Desktop alternative. It manages containers, images, volumes, networks, and Compose stacks from a clean, themeable browser UI in ~15–30 MB of RAM, with no Electron and no login. Positioning: "the GUI Colima never had." Works with Colima (first-class), Docker Engine, OrbStack, Podman, and remote daemons.

## Key facts

- License: Apache-2.0 (free and open source)
- Platforms: macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel), Linux (amd64 + arm64)
- Footprint: ~11 MB static binary, ~15–30 MB RAM, no Electron, no account
- Install: `brew install ParadoxInfinite/oriel/oriel` · a checksum-verified curl script · or `go install github.com/ParadoxInfinite/oriel@latest`
- Security: no built-in auth; driving Docker is root-equivalent, so run on 127.0.0.1 or a private network only, never the public internet
- Repository: https://github.com/ParadoxInfinite/oriel

## Docs

- [README, overview, install, features, comparison, FAQ](https://github.com/ParadoxInfinite/oriel/blob/main/README.md)
- [Supported engines (Colima, Docker, OrbStack, Podman, remote)](https://github.com/ParadoxInfinite/oriel/blob/main/docs/DAEMONS.md)
- [Remote access and reverse-proxy setup](https://github.com/ParadoxInfinite/oriel/blob/main/docs/REVERSE-PROXY.md)
- [Editions and theming](https://github.com/ParadoxInfinite/oriel/blob/main/docs/THEMES.md)
- [Security and trust model](https://github.com/ParadoxInfinite/oriel/blob/main/SECURITY.md)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/ParadoxInfinite/oriel/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)
- [Contributing](https://github.com/ParadoxInfinite/oriel/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)

## How Oriel compares

- vs Docker Desktop: Oriel is free/Apache-2.0 and lightweight; Docker Desktop is proprietary and paid for larger organizations.
- vs OrbStack: both are fast and light, but OrbStack is proprietary and paid for commercial use; Oriel is open source and brings your own engine.
- vs lazydocker: lazydocker is a terminal UI; Oriel is a real graphical web UI.
- vs Portainer: Portainer is a server you deploy in a container (needs ≥2 GB RAM); Oriel is a single local binary.
